root_cause.text: The application uses SnakeYAML's default constructor to parse YAML content, which allows the unsafe deserialization of arbitrary Java types and can lead to remote code execution.
root_cause.evidence: The patch restricts YAML parsing to safe standard types by replacing `new org.yaml.snakeyaml.Yaml()` with `new org.yaml.snakeyaml.Yaml(new SafeConstructor())` in the `readYamlTree` and `readYamlValue` methods.

agent_logs.root_cause_candidate: The application uses SnakeYAML's default constructor to parse YAML content, which allows the unsafe deserialization of arbitrary Java types and can lead to remote code execution.
agent_logs.evidence_points: The patch restricts YAML parsing to safe standard types by replacing `new org.yaml.snakeyaml.Yaml()` with `new org.yaml.snakeyaml.Yaml(new SafeConstructor())` in the `readYamlTree` and `readYamlValue` methods.

intent_record.intent_summary: Add SafeConstructor import to enable secure YAML parsing.
intent_record.what: Added an import statement for SafeConstructor.
intent_record.how: Provides the necessary class dependency for safely parsing YAML data.

intent_record.intent_summary: Secure YAML deserialization by enforcing SafeConstructor to prevent arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities.
intent_record.what: Modified Yaml object instantiations to include a SafeConstructor argument.
intent_record.how: Restricts the YAML parser to only construct safe, standard Java objects during deserialization, preventing arbitrary class instantiation.

grouping_result.intent: Secure YAML deserialization by enforcing SafeConstructor to prevent arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities.

consistency_review.core_intent: Secure YAML deserialization by implementing SafeConstructor to prevent arbitrary code execution vulnerabilities.

relevance_review.reason: The group directly addresses the root cause by replacing the unsafe default SnakeYAML constructor with SafeConstructor, preventing the arbitrary code execution vulnerability.

candidates.rationale: This line instantiates SnakeYAML with the default constructor, which allows unsafe deserialization of arbitrary Java types, leading to the vulnerability.

vuln_statements.explanation: This statement instantiates SnakeYAML using the default constructor, which allows unsafe deserialization of arbitrary Java types, directly causing the vulnerability.

bic_trace_step.commit_msg: Refs #146 - Yaml anchor refs parsing. Direct usage of snakeyaml
bic_trace_step.code_snippet:
commit 4044ecfb80732b721ffa206388574cf08bf7d295
Author: frantuma <frantuma@yahoo.com>
Date:   Mon Feb 1 18:01:34 2016 +0100

    Refs #146 - Yaml anchor refs parsing. Direct usage of snakeyaml

diff --git a/modules/swagger-parser/src/main/java/io/swagger/parser/util/DeserializationUtils.java b/modules/swagger-parser/src/main/java/io/swagger/parser/util/DeserializationUtils.java
index 6455f647..3e89ae09 100644
--- a/modules/swagger-parser/src/main/java/io/swagger/parser/util/DeserializationUtils.
bic_trace_step.reason: The commit introduces `readYamlTree` and `readYamlValue` methods which use the default constructor `new org.yaml.snakeyaml.Yaml()` followed by `yaml.load(contents)`. This is the exact root cause of the unsafe deserialization vulnerability.
